activities_projects.title: Listing Rani Abbakka’s Qualities
activities_projects.objective: Identify basic qualities of Rani Abbakka
steps: Read the chapter carefully
Underline or list words that describe Rani Abbakka
Write a short list of her qualities in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize bravery, intelligence, and leadership

activities_projects.title: Simple Summary Writing
activities_projects.objective: Summarize the chapter in a few sentences
steps: Write 5–6 sentences summarizing the story of Rani Abbakka
Include her main actions and achievements
Share your summary with a classmate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve basic comprehension and summarizing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Key Events
steps: List important events in Rani Abbakka’s life
Arrange them in the order they happened
Draw a simple timeline with dates or event names
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn chronological ordering of events
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct order and completeness of events (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion on Bravery
steps: Discuss in pairs or small groups why Rani Abbakka is considered brave
Give examples from the chapter
Write 2–3 sentences about your discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of bravery and leadership
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
activities_projects.objective: Act out a scene from the chapter
steps: Pick a simple scene from the chapter
Assign roles like Rani Abbakka and soldiers
Act out the scene for the class

activities_projects.title: Short Paragraph Writing
activities_projects.objective: Write about a day in Rani Abbakka’s life
steps: Imagine a day in the life of Rani Abbakka
Write 5–6 sentences describing her work and bravery
Include one challenge she faces
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop simple creative writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Content, clarity, and sentence structure (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Matching Exercise
steps: Write down 5 events from the chapter in Column A
Write their outcomes in Column B
Match them correctly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve understanding of cause-effect relationships

activities_projects.title: Identify Key Words
activities_projects.objective: Recognize important words in the chapter
steps: Underline key words related to bravery, leadership, and battles
Write them in your notebook with meanings
Use each word in a sentence
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and sentence usage (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simple Question-Answer
steps: Answer 5–6 simple questions from the chapter
Questions can include: Who is Rani Abbakka? Name one battle she fought. What makes her brave?
Write answers in full sentences
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of answers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Draw and Describe
steps: Draw a simple picture of Rani Abbakka leading her soldiers or preparing for battle
Write 2–3 sentences describing the scene
Share with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect creativity with understanding the story
